summary: Problem q1 asks to prove equivalence (mutual absolute continuity) of the Φ⁴₃ measure on 𝕋³ under translation by a smooth, non-zero shift T_ψ — a frontier quasi-invariance result of Cameron–Martin type. The current state shows no completed proof: the only solver attempt errored without producing a solution.tex, and the 'best proof' carries 100 open issues, indicating no viable proof skeleton yet exists. Solvability is assessed at 19%, reflecting the genuine technical depth required around renormalization and Girsanov-type changes of measure in the singular Φ⁴₃ setting.
problems: No solution.tex was ever generated: the interactive solve run terminated with an error, leaving the proof entirely unstarted.
The flagged 'best proof' is associated with 100 open issues, signaling it is not a coherent or near-complete argument.

Core mathematical blocker: establishing the Cameron–Martin/quasi-invariance shift requires controlling the singular Φ⁴₃ interaction under renormalization, where the naive Radon–Nikodym density is ill-defined and needs renormalized counterterms.

The shift T_ψ by a smooth ψ must be shown to lie in (or be compatible with) the effective Cameron–Martin space of the Gaussian reference measure, with the cubic/quartic cross terms requiring careful Wick renormalization.
Experiment provenance and model attribution are inconsistent/unknown ('Experiment: ?', differing model labels), making it hard to assess what was actually tried.
The resolved issue is only resolved in the bookkeeping sense (run errored), not because the mathematics was settled.
highlights: The expected answer direction is clear and correct: the measures should be equivalent, via a Cameron–Martin / Girsanov-type argument, giving a definite target for the proof.
The problem is well-posed with a concrete setting (unit torus 𝕋³, smooth non-zero ψ, translation operator T_ψ), enabling a structured decomposition into Gaussian-shift plus interaction-term analysis.
Strong analogy to existing literature (Hairer–Steele and related quasi-invariance results for Φ⁴ measures) provides a credible proof template to adapt.

suggested_todos.title: Produce an initial solution.tex skeleton structuring the proof as Gaussian Cameron–Martin shift plus renormalized interaction correction
Verify and document that smooth ψ lies in the Cameron–Martin space of the GFF reference measure, computing the explicit shift density
Derive the renormalized Radon–Nikodym density for the Φ⁴₃ interaction under T_ψ, identifying and handling the divergent Wick/counterterm contributions
Re-run the solver with the error-causing condition diagnosed and fixed so a complete attempt can actually be generated
Triage the 100 open issues on the best proof to separate substantive mathematical gaps from formatting/bookkeeping noise
Reconcile experiment/model provenance metadata so future attempts are correctly attributed
